item.title: Eli Lilly's Foundayo (tirzepatide)
item.description:
• Summary: The FDA has requested additional safety information from Eli Lilly for its obesity drug Foundayo (tirzepatide), delaying its key launch. The agency is seeking more data to assess potential heart and liver risks associated with the drug.
  • MoAs: Tirzepatide is a dual glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide (GIP) and glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) receptor agonist.
  • Sources: Fierce Pharma, Endpoints News

item.title: Eli Lilly Oral GLP-1 Preparation
item.description:
• Summary: Eli Lilly is investing billions to scale manufacturing and distribution for the anticipated overseas launch of its oral GLP-1 receptor agonists, targeting the growing global demand for diabetes and obesity treatments.
  • MoAs: GLP-1 receptor agonists mimic glucagon-like peptide-1 to increase insulin secretion, suppress glucagon release, and promote satiety.
  • Sources: BioSpace
